name: Srinidhi C.V
focus: Anxiety, depression, OCD, addiction, grief, relationship concerns, and neurorehabilitation.
bio: With an RCI license and MPhil in Clinical Psychology, I am a practising psychotherapist and a guest lecturer at Madras University. I have diverse experience, including working in Employee Wellness Programmes, Mental Health Organisations and leading a Neurodivergence division in a preschool. My therapy clientele includes issues around anxiety, depression, OCD, addiction, grief, relationship issues and neurorehabilitation for adult ADHD and brain trauma.

I hold a Diploma in Transactional Analysis, and I am currently training in TA and Gestalt Therapy. A trained practitioner in rational emotive cognitive behaviour therapy and solution-focused brief therapy, I prefer to utilise the humanistic, mindfulness and existential schools of thought, while integrating techniques from other schools depending on the client.

True to Rogers' words - "What you are to be, you are now becoming" - I believe by reaching out to a therapist, all of my clients are already on the path towards self-discovery and mastery. I also conduct experiential workshops to foster community building and interpersonal healing.
I am sex, body and animal positive, with a sprinkle of feminism and humour. I love art in all its forms - nature, music, dance, poetry, paintings, plays, and even memes.

name: Kalaivani Kumar
focus: Clinical psychotherapy, child intervention, parental counselling, and inclusive mental health outreach.
bio: Hello! I am Kalaivani, a practicing psychotherapist and psychologist with a Master's degree in Clinical Psychology and additional certifications in Rational Emotive Behavior Therapy, Emotion Focused Therapy, Cognitive Therapy, and Gestalt Therapy. I have dedicated myself to deepening my understanding of human behavior through 3 years of training in Transactional Analysis.

My therapy clientele includes concerns around anxiety, depression, addiction, attachment issues, grief, relationship issues, sexual abuse, and neurorehabilitation for children with autism. I prefer a client-centered, humanistic, and tailor-made approach that integrates different schools of thought depending on each client's need.

I have 5+ years of experience working with children with various special needs, planning interventions, parental counselling, and organizing workshops for parents and teachers in more than 10 schools.

Deeply passionate about dance and inclusivity, I founded Moneeth Inclusive Dance School to blend movement and mental wellbeing while promoting acceptance and community through the arts. With a mission to make psychology accessible, I also started the Instagram platform Ulaviyal Thirai to spread awareness about mental health and the importance of therapy.

name: Subha Joshi
focus: Adolescents and young adults; trauma-informed and integrative psychotherapy.
bio: With an MSc in Counselling Psychology, I am a practising psychotherapist with over 3 years of experience. I have worked with organisations in Chennai and provide therapy remotely. I primarily work with adolescents and young adults, supporting anxiety, depression, self-esteem struggles, grief, interpersonal conflicts, and trauma.

My approach is integrative and trauma informed. I draw from modalities such as Coherence Therapy, Internal Family Systems (IFS), AEDP, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), Cognitive Behaviour Therapy (CBT), and Narrative Therapy. Rather than following one fixed approach, I adapt my work based on the person in front of me and what they need.

Over time, my work has led me to a core understanding that people are not inherently wrong, faulty, or broken. There is always a context to our struggles. Often, beneath the pain, we are trying our best to protect ourselves. I try to hold space for all parts of a person with that understanding.

I focus on helping individuals connect more deeply with themselves and their inner experiences so we can understand their difficulties differently and move toward meaningful change. Outside of work, I enjoy listening to Taylor Swift, spending time in nature, and watching period dramas.

name: Cauviya Ravichandran
focus: Life transitions, relationship dynamics, emotional wellbeing, and youth mental health support.
bio: In my work as a counseling psychologist, I specialize in supporting individuals and young adults through life's transitions, relationship dynamics, and emotional challenges. My approach is rooted in genuine curiosity, active listening, and the belief that everyone's story matters.

I draw from person-centered, cognitive-behavioral, and mindfulness practices, always tailoring my methods to fit each client's unique needs. Beyond one-on-one sessions, I channel my passion for psychology into meaningful mental health content - articles, digital resources, and social media posts designed to demystify therapy and spark self-reflection.
Whether I am writing about modern psychological topics or developing practical tools, my focus is on clarity, warmth, and empowering others to take small yet impactful steps toward well-being.
Art, poetry, and nature are my anchors outside my professional life. For me, healing is a collaborative journey built on compassion, insight, and the everyday courage to change.

name: Gopi Krishna
focus: Stress, anxiety, relationships, interpersonal difficulties, attachment issues, emotional regulation, self-esteem, life transitions, and personal growth.
bio: With a Master's degree in Counselling Psychology from Christ University, I am a Counselling Psychologist and Psychotherapist with over five years of experience working with individuals across diverse psychological concerns in both clinical and corporate mental health settings. I currently work as a Corporate Psychologist within an Employee Assistance Program (EAP), supporting employees in navigating personal, emotional, and workplace-related challenges. My professional experience also includes a year-long internship in a school setting, providing counselling to children, adolescents, and their families.

I primarily work with young adults and middle-aged individuals, helping them navigate concerns such as stress, anxiety, relationship and interpersonal difficulties, attachment-related issues, emotional regulation, self-esteem, life transitions, and personal growth.

My therapeutic approach is rooted in Person-Centred Therapy, Gestalt Therapy, and an Eclectic framework, while also drawing insights from Jungian psychology when appropriate. I believe that every individual has the capacity for growth when provided with a safe, empathetic, and non-judgmental therapeutic space. Unconditional Positive Regard is a fundamental value in my practice and remains non-negotiable in every therapeutic relationship.
My aim is to collaborate with clients in developing greater self-awareness, emotional resilience, and meaningful change, while respecting each person's unique experiences, values, and pace of growth.

Outside the therapy room, I enjoy bringing humour into my everyday life and value the role it plays in fostering connection and resilience. I'm an avid cricket player and enjoy following sports such as football, tennis, and Formula 1. I also love watching films across a variety of genres, and if there's one thing that always brings me joy, it's cats—I'm an unapologetic cat lover.
